title: The Final Ingredient
theme: Eid celebration — the finder recovers the last fragment of Khala's stolen biryani recipe and completes the mission

content_brief.tone_notes: Fun, celebratory, and light. This is the payoff cache — the finder has followed the full trail and deserves a triumphant, festive finish. Eid framing should feel warm and inclusive, suitable for kids and families. Difficulty 1 means the narrative can be direct and rewarding, no misdirection. Terrain 3 reflects a physical challenge to reach the hide (writer may acknowledge 'you had to work for it') but keep the language encouraging, not intimidating.

series_context.series_note: This is the THIRD and FINAL cache in the Khala's Recipe trilogy. KGB-003 ('The Missing Shipment') was a standalone introductory drop. KGB-004 ('The Pigeon Drop') established the biryani spice / Khala's recipe storyline and explicitly promised a third cache. THIS cache is the resolution: the finder retrieves the last missing ingredient or recipe fragment, the mission is declared complete, and the celebration begins. The content must deliver a satisfying narrative close — no loose threads, no teaser for a future cache.

must_include: An opening that acknowledges the finder has completed the full series — they are a fully verified KGB field agent
Explicit resolution of the Khala's recipe storyline: the final spice or sealed recipe card is recovered, the biryani can now be made
Eid celebration framing — the recovered recipe is 'just in time for Eid', the mission was always about the feast
A congratulatory tone befitting a series finale — the KGB bureau thanks the agent by name (or alias) for their service
A kid-friendly flourish: something playful tied to Eid (e.g., mithai, Eid Mubarak, the smell of biryani wafting from HQ)
Standard field-agent close: sign the logbook, rehide carefully, and report the mission complete to KGB-HQ
must_avoid: Any teaser or hint at a fourth cache or future series — this is a definitive ending
Naming real public locations, restaurants, or landmarks
Content that requires the finder to have found KGB-003 or KGB-004 to understand — the finale should feel complete even for a solo find, while rewarding those who did the full trail
Dark or tense spy jargon — the register is celebratory, not classified-document bureaucratic
Excluding or alienating non-Muslim finders: Eid framing should feel like a warm family celebration, universally joyful
